name: run-tests
description: Run a repo's tests in a given directory, auto-detecting Go (go.mod), Django (runtests.py) vs pytest. Checks pytest availability before running.

tests.description: Test target(s): pytest path/node-id(s), Go package path(s) (e.g. './...'), or for Django a space-separated list of dotted test labels (e.g. 'auth.tests.test_x'). Empty runs the default suite.

cwd.description: Directory to run the tests in (repo root or subdir). Defaults to the agent's current working directory.

extra_args.description: Optional extra CLI args passed through to the underlying runner.

name: read-lines
description: Read lines from one or more files with optional head/tail/line-range and line numbering. Accepts a single 'file' string or a 'files' array to batch-read multiple files in one step. Defaults to first 50 lines per file.

file.description: Single file path to read (alternative to 'files').

files.description: Array of file paths to read in a single batch (alternative to 'file').

head.description: First N lines to show per file. Defaults to 50 if no head/tail/lines/start given.

tail.description: Last N lines to show per file.

lines.description: Line range as 'start-end' (e.g. '100-130'), 1-indexed inclusive. Preferred over separate start/end. Use with number=true for line-numbered output.

start.description: Starting line number (1-indexed, inclusive). Alternative to 'lines' string.

end.description: Ending line number (inclusive). Use with 'start' as alternative to 'lines'.

number.description: Prefix each line with its line number (like nl -ba or cat -n). Helps reference lines in subsequent edit calls.

name: search-code
description: Search for a regex pattern in file contents across a directory tree, optionally filtering by file extension glob. Returns file:line:content matches.

path.description: Root directory to search (default: current working directory).

include.description: Only search files whose relative path matches this glob (e.g. '*.py', '*_test.py', 'src/*.py'). Uses fnmatch syntax (supports * and ? wildcards, not brace expansion).

max_results.description: Maximum number of matching lines to return (default 50, -1 for unlimited).

name: find-files
description: Find files and/or directories matching a glob pattern within a directory tree, with optional depth limit.

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match (e.g. '*.py', '**/models.py'). Defaults to '*' if omitted.

path.description: Root directory to search (default: current working directory).

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 50, -1 for unlimited).

type.description: Type to search: 'files' or 'f' (default), 'dirs' or 'd', or 'both'.

max_depth.description: Maximum directory depth to traverse (default: unlimited). Use 1 for current dir only, 2 for current + immediate children, etc.

name: edit-file
description: Perform a string replacement in a file using Python str.replace (not regex). Replaces only the first occurrence. Returns the lines changed.

file.description: Path to the file to edit.

old_string.description: The exact string to be replaced.

name: run-python
description: Execute Python code inline. Writes code to a temp file, runs it with sys.executable, and returns stdout+stderr. Consolidates multi-step data analysis into one call.

code.description: Python source code to execute. Can include imports, file I/O, print statements — everything a standalone .py file would contain.

args.description: Optional command-line arguments passed to the Python script (sys.argv).
